mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy and reduced real earnings. This effect is persistent across market cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL over a 3-year rolling window, or the correlation turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to decreased consumer spending and corporate profits, thus driving S&P 500 forward returns lower over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate resilient consumer demand and economic growth, boosting corporate revenues and investor confidence, which drives S&P 500 forward returns higher. This joint strength signals broad economic health.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 2%
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show positive growth as specified, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of occurrences over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising federal funds rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs for corporations and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL is above 3%, a rise in FEDFUNDS does not lead to statistically significant negative 60-day SPX returns (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and robust supply-side capacity, translating into higher corporate output and earnings, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO month-over-month growth and SPX 5-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payroll employment growth signals a cooling labor market, which can precede a slowdown in consumer spending and broader economic activity, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 100k over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth indicates a strong overall economy, leading to higher corporate revenues and profits across sectors, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence remains high and economic uncertainty is low.
failure_condition: The annualized growth rate of GDPC1 shows no statistically significant positive correlation with SPX 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is low, a declining federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating growth, which reduces the cost of capital and boosts corporate valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL is below 2%, a fall in FEDFUNDS does not lead to statistically significant positive 20-day SPX returns (p-value > 0.1).
